title: phanerophlebia nephrolepioides
intro: Phanerophlebia nephrolepioides, also known as the Walking Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Africa, where it is commonly found growing on rocks and trees. The medicinal properties of Phanerophlebia nephrolepioides include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced skin health.
